original_hypothesis.title: FixedTermLoanHooks addRoleProvider() external call to provider.isPullProvider() can be abused to revert and block provider onboarding (admin DoS)
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: FixedTermLoanHooks.addRoleProvider performs an external call to IRoleProvider(providerAddress).isPullProvider() before storing the provider. A malicious providerAddress contract can revert or consume gas, causing addRoleProvider to revert and preventing the borrower from onboarding that provider (or any contract at that address).
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: This is mostly an admin/operational DoS and requires borrower action (choosing malicious provider address). Still, it can cause governance/process disruption if providers are dynamically discovered or addresses are reused or if a provider is upgraded externally to start reverting.
deployment_assumptions: Borrower attempts to add a providerAddress that is buggy/malicious or later changes behavior (e.g., metamorphic contract)
Operationally, adding/removing providers is critical for market access continuity
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: FixedTermLoanHooks.sol addRoleProvider calls external isPullProvider() with no try/catch; will bubble revert.

proof_of_signal.pattern: unguarded external call in admin setter; revert blocks state update

preconditions: providerAddress is a contract that reverts/consumes gas on isPullProvider()

initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: admin cannot add provider; potential operational outage if provider changes behavior
initial_code_evidence.missing_safeguards: no try/catch or low-level call fallback

analysis_questions: Is provider onboarding expected to be permissionless/third-party supplied, or only borrower-curated?
Should addRoleProvider tolerate failures and default to push-provider classification?
validation_requirements: Simulate provider that reverts on isPullProvider and confirm addRoleProvider reverts
Decide whether to harden with try/catch and treat revert as non-pull

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: Borrower attempts to add a provider address that is malicious/buggy, causing perpetual reverts, delaying or preventing access-control updates.

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: Indirect; inability to add providers can halt onboarding and thus deposits/withdrawals requiring credentials
potential_impact_if_confirmed.affected_users: Users awaiting credential support from new providers
potential_impact_if_confirmed.recovery_possibility: Borrower can select a different provider address

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: No tests for provider.isPullProvider() reverts in addRoleProvider

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
